name: Central Kurdish (Sorani)
notes: Central Kurdish / Sorani (کوردیی ناوەندی), the main Kurdish variety of Iraq and western Iran. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: the Kurdish Arabic alphabet, a Perso-Arabic script. Typologically an ABJAD by origin, but the Sorani orthography is close to fully alphabetic in practice: most vowels are written with letters (ا ە ێ ی ۆ و وو), with the short /i/ the main systematically unwritten vowel — which is exactly the part that requires lexical knowledge to restore, so no honest letter-to-IPA table can be given here without also modelling that. A full spec would also need contextual letter forms (initial/medial/final/isolated) to be normalised first, the ڕ/ر (trill/tap) and ڵ/ل (velarised/plain lateral) contrasts, and the ezafe. Sorani is also written in a Latin alphabet; input is assumed to be the Arabic-script orthography.
